metadata.name: clob
metadata.description: Central limit orderbook DEX on Solana (learning project)

docs: A central limit orderbook (CLOB) spot exchange.
Milestone 1: markets, vaults, balances, post-only orders, cancels.
Milestone 2 (this code): taker matching, taker fees, fill-event queue
and the permissionless consume_events settlement crank.
Milestone 3: indexer, trading UI.
Milestone 4: perps (oracle, margin, funding, liquidation).

instructions.name: cancel_order
docs: Remove one of the caller's resting orders and unlock its funds.

instructions.name: consume_events
docs: Settle up to `limit` fill events against the maker OpenOrders
accounts supplied as remaining accounts. Permissionless — this is
the crank any liquid market needs someone (M3: our indexer) to
turn.

instructions.name: create_margin_account
docs: Create the caller's margin account for a perp market.

instructions.name: create_open_orders
docs: Create the caller's per-market balance account. Required once per
(wallet, market) before depositing or trading.

instructions.name: deposit
docs: Move tokens from the caller's wallet into a market vault and credit
their free balance. `amount` is in raw token atoms.

accounts.name: user_token
docs: The caller's token account for the same mint as the vault; the
token program itself rejects a mint mismatch on transfer.

instructions.name: deposit_collateral
docs: Move collateral tokens into the perp vault.

instructions.name: init_market
docs: Create a market for a base/quote mint pair, its two token vaults
and its two orderbook sides. Permissionless: anyone can create a
market for any mint pair (one market per pair, enforced by PDA).

instructions.name: init_perp_market
docs: Create a perp market margined in `collateral_mint`, its vault,
and register the payer as admin / oracle keeper.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Becomes the market admin — i.e. the oracle keeper.

instructions.name: liquidate
docs: Permissionless liquidation of any account below maintenance
margin; the liquidator earns half the penalty.

accounts.name: liquidator_token
docs: Liquidator's collateral-mint token account for the bounty.

instructions.name: open_position
docs: Trade `delta` base atoms (+ long, − short) against the oracle
price, netting into the caller's existing position.

instructions.name: place_order
docs: Place an order. `price` is in ticks, `qty` in base lots.
The order first matches against the opposite side as far as its
limit price allows (unless PostOnly). The taker's balance changes
settle immediately; every maker fill is pushed onto the event
queue for the consume_events crank. Any remainder rests on the
book (Limit/PostOnly) or is dropped (ImmediateOrCancel).

instructions.name: set_oracle_price
docs: Keeper-pushed oracle price (admin only). Pyth replaces this on
devnet — same freshness rule, different account to read.

instructions.name: update_funding
docs: Permissionless funding crank: accrue the skew-derived premium
into the market's cumulative funding index.

instructions.name: withdraw
docs: Debit the caller's free balance and move tokens from the market
vault back to their wallet. The vault is owned by the market PDA,
so the program signs the transfer with the market's seeds.

instructions.name: withdraw_collateral
docs: Withdraw free collateral; the remaining position must still
clear initial margin at the current oracle price.

errors.name: InvalidMarketParams
errors.msg: Tick size, lot size and fee must be valid

errors.name: InvalidOrderParams
errors.msg: Price and quantity must be greater than zero

errors.name: OrderNotFound
errors.msg: Order not found on the book

errors.name: WouldCross
errors.msg: Post-only order would cross the book

errors.name: InvalidVault
errors.msg: Vault does not belong to this market

errors.name: SelfTrade
errors.msg: Order would match against the caller's own resting order

errors.name: EventQueueFull
errors.msg: Event queue is full — run the consume_events crank

errors.name: OracleStale
errors.msg: Oracle price is stale — the keeper must push a fresh price

errors.name: PriceSlippage
errors.msg: Oracle price moved beyond the order's slippage limit

errors.name: BelowInitialMargin
errors.msg: Position would fall below initial margin

errors.name: NotLiquidatable
errors.msg: Account is above maintenance margin — not liquidatable

types.name: EventQueue
docs: Ring buffer of fills awaiting settlement by the crank.
Why this exists: when a taker matches a resting order, the maker's
proceeds must be credited to the maker's OpenOrders — but that account
was not (and cannot be) passed in the taker's transaction. Matching
therefore records the fill here, and anyone may later call
`consume_events` with a batch of maker OpenOrders accounts to apply
the credits. Same design as Serum/OpenBook.
If the queue fills up, matching aborts (`EventQueueFull`) until a
crank drains it — maker funds are never silently dropped.

fields.name: head
docs: Index of the oldest unconsumed event.

fields.name: count
docs: Number of unconsumed events.

fields.name: seq
docs: Total events ever pushed (next event's `seq`).

fields.name: count
docs: Number of fill events settled by this crank turn.

types.name: FillEvent
docs: One fill, as recorded by the matching engine for later settlement.
Field order is chosen so the struct packs with no implicit padding
(Pod requires a fixed, fully-specified layout): pubkeys first, then
u64s, then the flag byte plus explicit padding. 112 bytes.

fields.name: maker
docs: Wallet that owned the resting (maker) order.

fields.name: taker
docs: Wallet whose taker order caused the fill.

fields.name: price
docs: Fill price in ticks — always the *maker's* price.

fields.name: qty
docs: Fill quantity in base lots.

fields.name: seq
docs: Monotonic sequence number, unique within the market.

fields.name: taker_side
docs: Side of the *taker* (`SIDE_BID`/`SIDE_ASK`). The maker is by
definition on the opposite side; consume_events uses this to know
which balance to unlock and which to credit.

fields.name: premium_bps
docs: Skew-derived premium in bps/day at crank time.

fields.name: cum_funding
docs: Lifetime accumulator, quote atoms per whole base unit.

types.name: MarginAccount
docs: A user's cross-state for one perp market: collateral plus one net
position. PDA: `["margin", market, owner]`.
The account never stores unrealized PnL or unsettled funding — both
are derived from the market's current oracle price and funding
accumulator, and folded into `collateral` only when the position is
touched (trade, liquidation, withdraw). That makes "settle funding
first" the invariant every mutating instruction starts with.

fields.name: collateral
docs: Quote atoms backing the position (deposits ± realized PnL ±
settled funding − fees − penalties).

fields.name: base_position
docs: Base atoms; positive = long, negative = short.

fields.name: avg_entry_price
docs: Volume-weighted entry, quote atoms per whole base unit.

fields.name: last_cum_funding
docs: `PerpMarket::cum_funding` as of the last settlement.

types.name: Market
docs: One market per (base, quote) mint pair.
PDA: `["market", base_mint, quote_mint]` — deriving the address from
the mint pair is what makes markets unique and permissionless: nobody
has to maintain a registry, and the same pair can never be created
twice.
Units convention (same scheme Serum/OpenBook use):
- a *lot* is the smallest tradeable base quantity: `base_lot_size`
base atoms.
- a *tick* is the smallest price increment: `tick_size` quote atoms
paid per base lot.
- so an order of `qty` lots at `price` ticks moves
`qty * base_lot_size` base atoms against `qty * price * tick_size`
quote atoms — all integer math, no floats on-chain.

fields.name: base_vault
docs: Token vault holding all deposited base tokens. Its authority is
this market PDA, so only the program can move funds out.

fields.name: quote_vault
docs: Token vault holding all deposited quote tokens.

fields.name: bids
docs: Zero-copy orderbook side accounts.

fields.name: event_queue
docs: Zero-copy fill-event ring buffer, drained by the consume_events
crank (see `state/event_queue.rs` for why it exists).

fields.name: tick_size
docs: Quote atoms per (base lot × tick).

fields.name: base_lot_size
docs: Base atoms per lot.

fields.name: taker_fee_bps
docs: Taker fee in basis points, charged on quote notional per fill.

fields.name: fees_accrued
docs: Quote atoms collected from taker fees. Sits in the quote vault;
a fee-withdrawal authority is deliberately out of scope until M3.

fields.name: next_order_id
docs: Monotonic order id counter, unique within this market.

types.name: OpenOrders
docs: A user's balance sheet for one market.
PDA: `["open_orders", market, owner]` — one per (wallet, market).
Funds move through three stages:
wallet ──deposit──▶ free ──place order──▶ locked ──cancel──▶ free
and `free ──withdraw──▶ wallet`. The tokens themselves always sit in
the market vaults; these fields are the program's ledger over them.
Locked balances back resting orders, so they can never be withdrawn
out from under the book.

fields.name: base_free
docs: Base atoms available to withdraw or place asks with.

fields.name: base_locked
docs: Base atoms backing resting ask orders.

fields.name: quote_free
docs: Quote atoms available to withdraw or place bids with.

fields.name: quote_locked
docs: Quote atoms backing resting bid orders.

types.name: Order
docs: One resting order. 56 bytes, `Pod` so it can live in a zero-copy array.

fields.name: price
docs: In ticks (quote atoms per base lot = price × tick_size).

fields.name: qty
docs: Remaining quantity in base lots.

types.name: OrderBookSide
docs: One side of the book, kept sorted best-first with time priority
within a price level.
This is a *zero-copy* account: at ~7.2 KB it is too large to borsh
round-trip on every instruction within Solana's compute/stack budget,
so Anchor `bytemuck`-casts the raw account bytes in place instead
(that's also why every field must be `Pod`: fixed layout, no enums,
no Options, explicit padding).
A sorted array with linear shifts is O(n) per insert — fine for 128
orders and easy to reason about. Replacing it with a crit-bit tree /
slab like Serum's (thousands of orders, O(log n)) is a planned
milestone-2 exercise; the instruction interface won't change.

fields.name: side
docs: `SIDE_BID` or `SIDE_ASK`.

fields.name: taker_side
docs: 0 = bid, 1 = ask — the side of the *taker*.

fields.name: price
docs: Fill price in ticks (the maker's price).

fields.name: qty
docs: Fill quantity in base lots.

fields.name: taker_fee
docs: Taker fee for this fill, in quote atoms.

fields.name: side
docs: 0 = bid, 1 = ask

fields.name: price
docs: In ticks.

fields.name: qty
docs: In base lots.

types.name: OrderType
docs: How an incoming order interacts with the opposite side of the book.

types.name: PerpMarket
docs: A perpetual futures market, margined and settled in the quote
(collateral) currency. Positions fill against the oracle price
rather than an orderbook: the CLOB is milestones 1–3's lesson,
margin/funding/liquidation is this one's.
The oracle is a keeper-pushed price on the market account itself,
gated to `admin`. On devnet this becomes a Pyth price account read —
the field layout (price + publish time) deliberately mirrors it.

fields.name: oracle_price
docs: Quote atoms per whole base unit (e.g. 50 USDC = 50_000_000).

fields.name: cum_funding
docs: Lifetime funding accumulator: quote atoms per whole base unit.
A position's unsettled funding is `pos × (cum_funding −
last_cum_funding) / BASE_UNIT`; positive means longs pay.

fields.name: max_funding_bps
docs: Cap on the skew-derived funding premium, in bps per day.

fields.name: init_margin_bps
docs: Margin required to *open* (e.g. 1000 bps = 10x max leverage).

fields.name: maint_margin_bps
docs: Margin below which anyone may liquidate.

fields.name: liq_fee_bps
docs: Liquidation penalty on closed notional; half to the liquidator,
half to the protocol.

fields.name: long_oi
docs: Open interest per side, in base atoms. Their imbalance drives
the funding premium: crowded longs pay shorts and vice versa.

fields.name: oracle_price
docs: Quote atoms per whole base unit.

fields.name: delta
docs: Signed trade size in base atoms (+ long, − short).

fields.name: price
docs: Fill price (the oracle price), quote atoms per whole base unit.

fields.name: realized_pnl
docs: PnL realized by any reduced/closed portion, quote atoms.

fields.name: size_closed
docs: Base atoms closed (signed, the position that was wiped).

types.name: Side
docs: Which side of the book an order rests on / an instruction targets.
